title: JV-08 - Head-to-head model decision ADR and default eligibility gate

labels.name: priority:P0
labels.description: Must-have for the first usable product increment

labels.name: level: atomic
labels.description: Small independent execution slice that can be assigned directly

body:
## Summary

Synthesize Google, OPUS-MT, M2M100, and NLLB benchmark evidence into a default eligibility decision.

## Why is this needed?

Default local MT is a product decision with quality, latency, license, privacy, and release implications.

## Current context

This WBS comes from tentacle research on 2026-05-21. Current repo state: mt_provider still defaults to google; src/bin/mt_bench.rs emits a pending lf-04-v1 fixture; local OPUS-MT provider exists behind local-mt but lacks benchmark-proven realtime replacement evidence; config has explicit mt_cloud_fallback consent; #372 closed with routing/fixture only; #383 tracks 60fps render performance; PR #407 aligns docs with local-first STT but does not make local MT default.

## Inputs

- Artifacts from JV-04, JV-05, JV-06, JV-07.
- Model checklist from JV-01.
- Benchmark thresholds from ja-vi-mt-benchmark handoff.

## Outputs

- ADR in docs/evidence or docs/decisions selecting default candidate or keeping Google default.
- Machine-readable verdict with pass/fail per gate.
- Rejected alternatives and accepted tradeoffs.

## Implementation approach

- Apply gates: chrF++ local >= 0.90 x Google and >= 45.0, bootstrap CI lower bound >= -3 chrF, p95 <= 700ms, RTF <= 0.5, error <= 0.5%, RSS <= 1GB, CPU <= 60%, no silent fallback.
- If gates fail, keep local MT opt-in and create gap issues instead of flipping default.
- Require explicit human/product override for any quality tradeoff.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Decision confidence is 1.0 for issue routing even if model choice is defer/blocked.
- [ ] ADR names the chosen candidate or states why no candidate qualifies.
- [ ] Default flip criteria are objectively linked to artifacts.
- [ ] NFR and security reviews sign off on the decision.

## Test cases / evidence gates

- [ ] Artifact validator over every benchmark JSON.
- [ ] Scripted gate evaluator returns pass/fail.
- [ ] Review agent verdict CLEAN.

## Dependencies

- Depends on JV-01 through JV-07.
- Blocks JV-09 through JV-21 default-release path if no candidate qualifies.

## Agent routing

Architecture analyst on opus-class; nfr-verification-gate mandatory; user/product override required for quality tradeoff.

## Project / WBS

- WBS key: JV-08
- Wave: S1 selection
- Project target: magicpro97/project/2
- Labels: type: architecture, area: providers, area: verification, priority:P0, level: atomic

## Duplicate / extension note

Supersedes the conditional default-flip part of #372 with real evidence.
